zh.All Cannings, Wiltshire, England: 英國維爾爾哥吉拉 卡寧斯
zh.lifeSummary: Isabel Elizabeth Marr於大約1515年出生在英國維爾爾哥吉拉 卡寧斯。她於1530年與Sir Henri Hiscoxe  Hiscockes在英國英格蘭維爾爾哥吉拉 卡寧斯結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1569年5月5日在英國英格蘭維爾爾哥吉拉 卡寧斯享年55歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭韋斯特 越可爾阿爾蒙德波利哥吉拉 哈洛福也 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Isabel Elizabeth Marr于大约1515年出生在英国維爾爾哥吉拉 卡寧斯。她于1530年与Sir Henri Hiscoxe  Hiscockes在英国英格兰維爾爾哥吉拉 卡寧斯结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1569年5月5日在英国英格兰維爾爾哥吉拉 卡寧斯享年55岁，埋葬在英国英格兰韋斯特 越可爾阿爾蒙德波利哥吉拉 哈洛福也 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Marr
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the district of Mar in Aberdeenshire, an ancient tribal name probably based on a Brittonic personal name Marsos. There may have been confusion with Mair 2.
English: habitational name from one or other of various places called from Old Norse marr ‘fen, marsh’, such as Marr, High and Low Marr in Wheldrake, and The Marrs in Swine (all Yorkshire).
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Marro.

variants.name: Marrs
meanings: English: variant of Marr , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Morr
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of German   Marr   3.

variants.name: Mair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Franz, Alois, Benno, Egon, Hans, Hermann, Inge, Kurt, Othmar, Otto, Reinhard, Rudi.

Scottish: occupational name from Gaelic maor ‘officer’, used specifically for an officer who executed summonses and other legal writs, including not only the king's herald or sergeant but also head foresters and the like. See also Maver and compare Mayer 3.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: Isabel Elizabeth Marr was born about 1515, in All Cannings, Wiltshire, England. She married Sir Henri Hiscoxe  Hiscockes in 1530, in All Cannings, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 5 May 1569, in All Cannings, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 55, and was buried in All Hallows' Church, Almondbury, West Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Elizabeth Marrは約1515年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアアル カニングズで生まれた。1530年にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアアル カニングズでSir Henri Hiscoxe  Hiscockesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1569年5月5日にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアアル カニングズで55歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウエスト ヨークシュアイアアルモンドブリーアル ハロウス' チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.All Hallows' Church, Almondbury, Metropo: 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 요르크실르 알몽버이 앨 핼로스' c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Elizabeth Marr(은)는 대략 1515년 영국 윌츠히르 앨 캔잉스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1530년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 앨 캔잉스에서 Sir Henri Hiscoxe  Hiscockes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1569년 5월 5일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 앨 캔잉스에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 요르크실르 알몽버이 앨 핼로스' c후르치에 묻혔다.

ru.All Hallows' Church, Almondbury, Metropo: алл холлоус' чурч, алмондбури, вест еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Isabel Elizabeth Marr родилась около 1515 г. в местности алл каннингс, уилцхир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Henri Hiscoxe  Hiscockes в 1530 г. в местности алл каннингс, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 5 мая 1569 г. в местности алл каннингс, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 55 лет и была похоронена в местности алл холлоус' чурч, алмондбури, вест еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
